description: Move existing syscall.Exec-based implementations of attachToTmuxSession, execAgent, execRuntime from helpers.go into helpers_unix.go behind //go:build !windows tag. The shared helper signatures stay in helpers.go; platform-specific exec logic goes into the platform files.
title: Create helpers_unix.go with build tags

description: Windows implementations of attachToTmuxSession, execAgent, execRuntime using os/exec.Command().Run() with stdin/stdout/stderr passthrough and exit code propagation. Replace syscall.Exec which is Unix-only. File: internal/cmd/helpers_windows.go with //go:build windows tag.
title: Create helpers_windows.go for Windows exec support

description: Multiple Go files bypass the OS-aware helpers (config.ExportPrefix, config.PrependEnv) and hardcode bash export VAR=value syntax, which breaks on Windows PowerShell. Files: handoff.go (builds exports[] manually), quota/executor.go, cmd/quota.go, cmd/rig_detect.go, cmd/role.go, cmd/worktree.go, hooks/config.go (pathSetup). Each needs to either use the existing helpers or add runtime.GOOS checks.
title: Fix hardcoded export syntax for Windows compatibility

daemon/ package has ~35 standalone exec.Command calls in background context without SetProcessGroup. These run on daemon tick loops and cause rapid window flashing. Files and key lines:
- lifecycle.go: lines 43, 634, 656, 674, 699, 718, 758, 796, 864, 955, 962, 1132, 1255 (mail send, git fetch/pull/stash, bd show/list)
- checkpoint_dog.go: line 164 (git commands)
- dog_molecule.go: line 278 (bd commands)
- dolt_backup.go: lines 114, 149, 191 (dolt backup)
- dolt_remotes.go: lines 149, 174, 228, 247, 266 (dolt sql)
- jsonl_git_backup.go: lines 300, 308, 417, 426, 440, 460, 538 (git/dolt/gt commands)
- main_branch_test_runner.go: lines 212, 221, 228, 236, 268 (git worktree)
- scheduled_maintenance.go: line 205 (gt maintain)
- wisp_reaper.go: line 129 (gt commands)
- pressure_darwin.go: lines 11, 32, 48 (sysctl/vm_stat — darwin only, low priority)
Fix: add util.SetProcessGroup(cmd) after each exec.Command call.
title: Add SetProcessGroup to all exec.Command calls in daemon/ package

description:

dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

git/git.go lines 93 and 115: the central run() and runWithEnv() methods create exec.Command without calling util.SetProcessGroup(cmd). ALL Git struct method calls flow through these two functions. Fix: add 'util.SetProcessGroup(cmd)' after each exec.Command call. git.go does not currently import util — need to add import. No circular dependency risk (util doesn't import git). This single fix cascades to ~25+ call sites.
title: Add SetProcessGroup to git.run() and git.runWithEnv() central runners

close_reason: Fixed by epic gt-th7: all exec.Command calls now have SetProcessGroup/CREATE_NO_WINDOW
description: After applying CREATE_NO_WINDOW to all gt subprocess spawns (66ef8d2), console windows still flash when a polecat session is running. The Go-side fix covers gt's own spawns, but the flashing likely originates from processes spawned INSIDE the polecat's tmux session (Copilot CLI, node, git, shell commands). Need to identify the exact source and suppress it.
title: Windows console flashing persists during polecat sessions

close_reason: Fixed by epic gt-th7: all exec.Command calls now have SetProcessGroup/CREATE_NO_WINDOW
description:
Verification of gt-brn found additional daemon-reachable exec.Command calls missing util.SetProcessGroup():

1. internal/util/exec.go ExecWithOutput() and ExecRun() - called by Witness (daemon context) to spawn bd, gt session restart, gt polecat nuke. HIGH flash risk.

2. internal/git/git.go run()/runWithEnv() - reached from daemon via Witness->Beads->Git and Daemon->KRCPruner->Git. HIGH flash risk.

3. internal/proxy/exec.go runCommand() - proxy server spawns gt/bd without CREATE_NO_WINDOW. Risk depends on how proxy is launched.

4. internal/plugin/recording.go RecordRun() - spawns bd close without the fix.

Fix: Add util.SetProcessGroup(cmd) to these locations, or refactor exec utility functions to always apply it on Windows.
title: fix(windows): suppress console flashing in util.ExecWithOutput/ExecRun and git.run

close_reason: Built with go build, verified clean compilation with go vet, tested all session commands on Windows. Binary installed to go/bin and pushed to remote.
description: make build on Windows, confirm binary compiles with new platform-split files. Run gt mayor at to verify the full flow works. This is the final verification step.
title: Build and verify Windows session-joining support

close_reason: All 7 sub-tasks completed. 173+ exec.Command calls across git, beads, daemon, refinery, doltserver, deacon, polecat, tui, and other background packages now have SetProcessGroup/CREATE_NO_WINDOW. Commits: 958f5ef, 0dd2877, 457421a, fb28a1d, 80e5fc3.
description: On Windows, exec.Command calls without CREATE_NO_WINDOW cause a visible console window to flash. The daemon/refinery/doltserver spawn processes multiple times per second, making the desktop unusable. 173 exec.Command calls in background-context packages are missing util.SetProcessGroup(cmd). Key areas: git.run(), beads.run(), daemon/, refinery/, doltserver/, deacon/. The util.SetProcessGroup helper already exists and sets CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP|CREATE_NO_WINDOW on Windows.
title: [epic] Fix Windows console window flashing from subprocess spawning

These are package-level functions like Clone (line 215), runMergeCheck (line 877), InitSubmodules (line 1805), InitSparseCheckout (line 1818), etc. Each needs util.SetProcessGroup(cmd) after exec.Command. Full list of lines: 215, 322, 353, 367, 372, 383, 391, 877, 1195, 1206, 1215, 1805, 1818, 1826, 1921, 1949, 1972, 1989, 2003, 2011.
title: Add SetProcessGroup to standalone git exec.Command calls in git/git.go

close_reason: Fixed in 373f850 - skip tmux #() on Windows
description: On Windows, tmux #() shell commands in status-right spawn visible cmd.exe + conhost.exe windows. Each session with '#(gt status-line --session=X 2>/dev/null)' flashes a console window every status-interval (5s). With 4+ sessions this causes near-constant flashing. The 2>/dev/null is also Unix-only syntax. Fix: either use a different mechanism for status-line on Windows, or suppress window creation (CREATE_NO_WINDOW flag).
title: Windows: tmux #() status-line commands flash cmd.exe windows
